title: The Service Firm Automation Readiness Score
subtitle: A 12-minute self-assessment for owners and ops leads at 11–100 employee service businesses. Find your weakest category.

questions.text: How consistently does a new client go from inquiry to kickoff without human data entry?

answers.label: 2 — Form submit is automated; rest is manual
3 — Some CRM auto-populate but gaps remain

questions.text: How fast does a new lead get a personalized first response?

answers.label: 1 — Days, when someone gets to it

questions.text: How much of the kickoff prep (scheduling, engagement letter, kickoff doc) is automated?

low.headline: Intake is eating 8-12 hrs/person/week
low.text: Every missing automation here compounds downstream. Start with form-to-CRM auto-population — it's a 2-day build that pays back in 30 days at your size.
steps: Wire your intake form (Tally or Typeform) directly to your CRM via n8n
Trigger a personalized welcome email (Claude-drafted, variable by industry) on form submit
Auto-schedule the kickoff via Cal.com round-robin, embedded in the welcome email

mid.headline: Intake is half-automated — the gaps compound
mid.text: The biggest leak is usually between form submit and engagement letter. Most firms have the front and back automated but re-type data in the middle.
steps: Audit where human retyping still happens (look for copy-paste in your team's screen recordings)
Add a Claude-powered 'field normalizer' that extracts engagement letter variables from the intake form
Auto-send e-sign via DocuSign or HelloSign as soon as the kickoff call is booked

high.headline: Intake is dialed — push into engagement depth
high.text: You're ahead of most service firms. Next move: personalize the onboarding sequence by industry or size cluster.
steps: Add persona branching in the welcome email based on intake form industry field
Generate an industry-specific onboarding deck automatically (Gamma API + Claude)
Measure form-to-kickoff-call conversion rate weekly in Supabase

questions.text: How consistently are weekly client status updates generated without manual writing?
How consistently does QA happen on deliverables before client sees them?

low.headline: Delivery ops is where margin vanishes
low.text: Manual status updates alone eat 2-4 hours per account per week. At 10 accounts, that's a part-time hire.
steps: Wire your PM tool (ClickUp / Linear / Asana) to a weekly status-update workflow using Claude
Auto-reconcile time entries to projects using a Supabase nightly job
Standardize a QA checklist that fires on deliverable state change

mid.headline: Solid delivery ops, one layer short
mid.text: You probably have status updates or reconciliation automated — rarely both. The gap compounds at scale.
steps: Close whichever is still manual (most firms have status auto, billing manual)
Add a Claude QA pass on every deliverable before send
Instrument client-satisfaction pulse after each major milestone

high.headline: Delivery is tight — focus on insight layer
high.text: You're running lean. Next leverage point: turn delivery data into predictive signals.
steps: Build a project-health dashboard predicting at-risk accounts
Auto-flag accounts with late deliverables for owner attention
Publish a quarterly delivery-ops benchmark for the firm

questions.text: How automated is your outbound prospecting research?
How consistently are proposals generated from intake / discovery data?
How systematically does your team follow up on stalled opportunities?

low.headline: Pipeline ops is pure friction
low.text: Most owner-led firms leak 30-40% of potential revenue to stalled follow-ups and slow proposals.
steps: Add a Claude-powered prospect research workflow that enriches LinkedIn profiles
Auto-draft proposals from discovery-call transcripts (Fathom + Claude)
Build a 45-day stalled-deal re-engagement cadence in your CRM

mid.headline: Sales ops half-built
mid.text: You probably have one of (research / proposals / follow-up) automated. The other two compound the pipeline leak.
steps: Close the weakest sub-process first
Instrument conversion-rate-by-stage in Supabase
Add a Friday 'stalled deals' digest to your inbox

high.headline: Sales ops is solid — focus on offer engineering
high.text: The automation layer is there. Next leverage: tune the offer, not the ops.
steps: A/B test proposal framing on comparable prospects
Track proposal-view telemetry (how long, what sections)
Move to productized offers with fixed scope + price

questions.text: How automated is invoice generation from completed milestones?
How consistently are collections (past-due follow-ups) automated?
How quickly does your leadership see monthly P&L after month-close?

low.headline: Finance ops is eating owner hours
low.text: Monthly close, invoicing, and collections are the #1 time drain for service-firm owners.
steps: Wire milestone-complete in PM tool → auto-invoice via QuickBooks / Xero API
Set up a 7 / 14 / 30-day past-due reminder cadence
Build a real-time P&L dashboard in Supabase

mid.headline: Partial finance automation
mid.text: Usually invoicing is automated but collections and close are manual. Collections is the biggest hidden drain.
steps: Automate past-due reminders with escalation path
Add a monthly-close checklist that the accountant runs in under 2 hours
Publish the P&L dashboard to leadership

high.headline: Finance is dialed — focus on forecasting
high.text: Automation is there. Next leverage: predictive cash-flow.
steps: Build a 13-week rolling cash-flow forecast
Tie pipeline data to revenue forecasts
Add an anomaly-detection layer on monthly spend

questions.text: How systematically is team training material captured and maintained?
How consistently does team knowledge persist when someone leaves?

low.headline: Knowledge is all in people's heads
low.text: When your best op leaves, 40% of what they knew leaves with them. Automation here is about institutional memory.
steps: Record every client call (Fathom / Fireflies) and index transcripts in a searchable Supabase table
Auto-generate SOPs from screen recordings using Claude
Build a Slack Claude bot that answers team questions from the knowledge base

mid.headline: Some knowledge systems, big gaps
mid.text: You probably have SOPs in a wiki somewhere but they rot. The fix is continuous update, not one-time documentation.
steps: Add a weekly 'SOP stale' check — flag docs unmodified >90 days
Auto-draft SOP updates from call transcripts of the SOP topic
Run a quarterly knowledge audit with the team

high.headline: Knowledge ops is strong — level up hiring
high.text: You've solved knowledge capture. Next: make hiring itself automation-enhanced.
steps: Build an AI-assisted screening pipeline for inbound applicants
Auto-generate a 30-60-90 day plan per new hire from their role + your stack
Track new-hire time-to-productivity as a KPI

cta.headline: Want help closing your weakest category?
cta.description: Book a 20-min working session. No pitch. You walk me through your top 3 bottlenecks and I'll tell you the one I'd attack first — free, based on your score.
